title: Health & Emergencies
shortSummary: Describe symptoms, ask for a pharmacy or doctor, and get help in an emergency.
culturalNote: In Brazil the emergency numbers are 192 for an ambulance (SAMU) and 190 for the police. For everyday ailments the first stop is the farmácia (pharmacy), which is plentiful and where pharmacists can advise on minor problems. To describe pain, Brazilians use estou com plus the symptom: estou com dor de cabeça (I have a headache, literally 'I'm with head pain'). Knowing preciso de um médico (I need a doctor) and chame uma ambulância (call an ambulance) is valuable. Brazilians are caring and will readily help someone who is unwell.

grammar.explanation: To say you have a symptom or feeling, Portuguese often uses estar com (to be with): estou com dor (I have pain), estou com fome (I'm hungry), estou com febre (I have a fever).
grammar.commonMistake: Translating 'I have' with tenho for symptoms; estar com sounds far more natural.

grammar.explanation: The verb precisar takes de before a noun: preciso de ajuda (I need help), preciso de um remédio (I need a medicine). Before a verb, it can drop the de: preciso descansar.
grammar.commonMistake: Dropping de before a noun. It is preciso de um médico, not preciso um médico.

dialogue.setup: A traveller feels unwell and speaks to a pharmacist.

lines.speaker: Farmacêutico
lines.native: Bom dia, em que posso ajudar?
lines.english: Good morning, how can I help?

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.native: Não estou me sentindo bem. Estou com dor de cabeça.
lines.english: I don't feel well. I have a headache.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.native: Um pouco. E sou alérgico a aspirina.
lines.english: A little. And I'm allergic to aspirin.

lines.speaker: Farmacêutico
lines.native: Tudo bem, vou te dar este aqui.
lines.english: Alright, I'll give you this one.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.english: Thank you. I need to rest.

lines.speaker: Farmacêutico
lines.native: Sim, e beba bastante água. Melhoras!
lines.english: Yes, and drink plenty of water. Get well!

quiz.question: How do you say 'I need a doctor' in Portuguese?
Which phrase means 'I have a headache'?
Preciso _____ ajuda. (preposition)
quiz.explanation: Precisar takes de before a noun.

quiz.question: Where would you go first for a minor ailment in Brazil?
quiz.explanation: The farmácia (pharmacy) handles minor health needs.

quiz.question: Arrange: uma / chame / ambulância
quiz.explanation: Chame uma ambulância means 'call an ambulance'.
